qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unsafe JSON deserialization in TCP microservice transport enables prototype pollution
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The advisory describes a HEAD request middleware bypass in @nestjs/platform-fastify's HTTP routing layer. This finding describes prototype pollution via unsanitized JSON.parse in the TCP microservice transport (tcp-socket.ts). The vulnerable code, root cause, transport layer, and vulnerability class are all different.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerable code or root cause. The advisory describes a HEAD request middleware bypass in @nestjs/platform-fastify where Fastify automatically redirects HEAD requests to GET handlers, skipping registered GET middleware. The only qualifying finding (tcp-microservice-proto-pollution) addresses prototype pollution in the TCP microservice transport, which is a different transport layer and different root cause.
evidence: Advisory root cause: Fastify HEAD-to-GET redirect bypasses GET middleware in @nestjs/platform-fastify (fixed in 11.1.16, CWE-670).
Finding tcp-microservice-proto-pollution root cause: JSON.parse without prototype pollution sanitization in TCP microservice transport (tcp-socket.ts).
The finding does not touch the Fastify HTTP adapter, HEAD request handling, or middleware pipeline.
